metadata.title:
I CLASH IN


COUNCIL

Y. w. c. A. TRIES TO CAPTURE
POLICE MATRoN ADVOCATED
BY WOMAN'S CLUB.

COUNCIL IS WITH CLUB

pages.raw_text:
The effort of the Young Women's
Christian association to step into
fight started by the Woman's club
and capture the police matron the
club had been advocating for some
time was rebuked by the represen-
tatives present at the city council
last night. The Y. w. c. A. wanted
to do the work at sos per month,
but the Woman's club leaders said
they wanted the city to make it's
own selection and fix the pay.


The council authorized the en-
ployment of one police matron at
this time, the question of compen-
sation to be left with the police
department.


Conditions in the Spokane city
jail and the handling of women
prisoners was scored more than
once, and at least three references
were made to the case of Agnes
Fair, the l. w. w. girl, who claimed
that one of the officers attempted
to outrage her while confined with
in a, cell in the city prison. 1t was
this charge of the Fair girl that led
the women to take action in the
matter.


The result of a ballot taken in
different churches and schools was
read. showing 1929 votes in favor
of the proposition OF the employ
ment of police matron.
Mrs. II. Nj. Prayer, recently a
candidate for membership on the
school board. made an effective
plea for a police matron. She said
that if the city used as diligent
efforts to keep women out of the
jailk that it does to paddock them
In When once they land there that
better results for the city at large
could be obtained.


Another woman speaker demand.
ed the appointment of police
matron in the name of common
decency. She spoke of the war on
the white slave traffic, and said
that 1t took more than policemen
With "lot of fine necks" to cope
with the question.


Sbme of the councilmen wanted
to fefer and postpone after the
usual manner, but Councilman
Dalke came to the aid Of the wo-
men, and the thing then went
through with whirl.
